title: Script file exceeds 5MB threshold indicating potential content multiplication
description: Script files exceeding 5MB are almost certainly corrupted by content multiplication (recursive paste, regex replacement loop, or tool duplication). Normal PowerShell scripts rarely exceed 500KB. The 296MB Main-GUI.ps1 incident (SIN-20260329-VERSIONTAG-CORRUPTION) was caused by exactly this pattern. Already checked by SyntaxGuard (check #1) but this SIN adds automated quarantine.
detection_method: File size check: Get-ChildItem -File | Where Length -gt 5MB
preventionRule: Any .ps1/.psm1 file over 5MB must be quarantined and reviewed. Over 20MB is auto-flagged as CRITICAL corruption.
remedy: Compare file against last known-good checkpoint. Trim duplicated content. Restore from epoch backup if available via PwShGUI-VersionManager.
